agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment with addresses, transition through states, charge based on weight bracket.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — owns shipment lifecycle (creation, state transitions, finality) and weight-based classification for billing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, with a defined weight and lifecycle state."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where the parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where the parcel is delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state after shipment creation. The shipment has been registered but not yet picked up or moving."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"The shipment is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal/final state. The shipment has reached its delivery address. No further state changes permitted."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive (>0) and cannot exceed 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"Classification tier derived from shipment weight that determines pricing: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no parcel movement without an origin and destination. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment with a missing or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions are strictly forward-only: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State regression would corrupt the shipment lifecycle record. A parcel that has left the origin cannot logically revert to not-yet-dispatched. This is a state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Transitioning a shipment from In Transit back to Pending must be rejected. Transitioning a shipment from Delivered to any other state must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state. Once a shipment reaches Delivered, no further state changes are allowed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery finality is a physical and contractual constraint — the parcel has been handed over. Changing state after delivery would corrupt audit trails and contractual records.","testable_assertion":"Any state change attempted on a Delivered shipment must be rejected with an error."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would break billing calculations. This is a physical reality constraint.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 1000 kg maximum is a business policy cap, not a physical law. Operations could raise this limit if the fleet adds heavier-capacity vehicles.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket classification: Standard for weight in (0, 10] kg, Heavy for weight in (10, 50] kg, Freight for weight in (50, 1000] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy. A VP could adjust thresholds (e.g., make Heavy start at 15 kg) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A 10 kg shipment must be classified as Standard. A 10.01 kg shipment must be classified as Heavy. A 50 kg shipment must be classified as Heavy. A 50.01 kg shipment must be classified as Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user provides a valid pickup address, delivery address, and positive weight <= 1000 kg","when":"The user creates a shipment","then":"The shipment is created in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user omits the pickup address or the delivery address","when":"The user attempts to create a shipment","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating the missing address"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A shipment exists in Pending state","when":"The shipment transitions to In Transit","then":"The state is updated to In Transit and the transition is recorded"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment exists in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition with an error indicating backward transitions are not allowed"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment exists in In Transit state","when":"The shipment transitions to Delivered","then":"The state is updated to Delivered and the transition is recorded as final"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment exists in Delivered state","when":"Any state change is attempted (to Pending, In Transit, or any other state)","then":"The system rejects the transition with an error indicating Delivered is a terminal state"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A user provides a weight of 0, a negative weight, or a weight exceeding 1000 kg","when":"The user attempts to create a shipment","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error about invalid weight"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment is created with weight 5 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Standard"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment is created with weight 25 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment is created with weight 75 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundary: 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Standard (upper-inclusive). The spec says '0-10 kg is standard', so 10 kg is Standard."},{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundary: 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy (upper-inclusive). The spec says '10-50 kg is heavy', so 50 kg is Heavy."},{"scenario":"Weight at exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight. 1000 kg is the maximum, not above it."},{"scenario":"Weight at fractional values near zero (e.g., 0.001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Accepted — weight is positive. System should define a practical minimum if sub-gram parcels are not operationally viable, but spec does not restrict this."},{"scenario":"Attempt to skip state: Pending directly to Delivered","expected_behavior":"Spec does not explicitly address this. Strict reading of forward-only implies Pending → In Transit → Delivered is the only valid path, so skipping In Transit should be rejected."},{"scenario":"Shipment creation with identical pickup and delivery address","expected_behavior":"Spec does not prohibit this. Technically valid, but operationally suspect. Recommend a warning or business validation rule in future."},{"scenario":"Weight update after shipment is In Transit or Delivered","expected_behavior":"Spec is silent on mutability of weight post-creation. Recommend treating weight as immutable once In Transit to preserve billing integrity."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight brackets directly drive pricing and revenue. Incorrect bracket assignment means under- or over-charging customers. The 1000 kg cap limits addressable market for very heavy shipments.","operational":"Strict state machine prevents operational confusion — dispatchers and drivers can trust the shipment status. Terminal Delivered state ensures clean handoff to invoicing and proof-of-delivery processes.","user":"Clear state transitions give customers reliable tracking. Weight validation at creation prevents downstream surprises (billing disputes, carrier rejections).","risk":"Low overall risk — the spec is clear and internally consistent. Primary risk is bracket boundary ambiguity (inclusive vs exclusive) which could cause billing disputes at exactly 10 kg and 50 kg. Secondary risk: the spec is silent on state-skip (Pending → Delivered) and weight mutability.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (shippers)","impact":"Weight validation and bracket classification directly affect what they are charged. State finality gives them confidence in delivery confirmation."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatchers","impact":"State machine integrity ensures they can trust shipment status for routing and scheduling decisions."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight brackets are the pricing input. Accuracy of bracket assignment is critical for revenue recognition."},{"group":"Drivers / Couriers","impact":"State transitions reflect their real-world actions (pickup → deliver). Backward transitions would create contradictory instructions."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is clear, concise, and internally consistent. No contradictions detected. Confidence is not higher because: (1) bracket boundary inclusivity is ambiguous — 'over 50 kg' is clear but '0-10 kg' and '10-50 kg' leave the exact boundary unclear, (2) the spec is silent on whether Pending → Delivered (state skip) is allowed, (3) no guidance on weight mutability after creation, (4) no explicit mention of address validation rules beyond presence.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine direction: forward-only, Delivered is terminal","Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg","Three weight brackets exist with the described ranges","Both addresses required at creation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact bracket boundary inclusivity at 10 kg and 50 kg","Whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is valid","Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation","What constitutes a valid address beyond non-empty"],"assumptions":["Bracket boundaries are upper-inclusive: Standard includes 10 kg, Heavy includes 50 kg, Freight includes 1000 kg","State transitions must follow strict sequence Pending → In Transit → Delivered with no skipping","Weight is immutable once shipment leaves Pending state","Addresses are validated only for presence, not format or geocoding"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Bracket boundary inclusivity is not explicitly defined. Does a 10 kg parcel fall into Standard or Heavy? The phrase '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy' creates overlap at exactly 10 kg and 50 kg.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Customers (shippers)"],"options":["A: Upper-inclusive (10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy) — natural reading of '0-10 kg'","B: Lower-inclusive (10 kg = Heavy, 50 kg = Freight) — natural reading of '10-50 kg'"],"recommendation":"Option A (upper-inclusive). '0-10 kg is standard' most naturally reads as 'up to and including 10 kg'. Recommend PM confirm."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Can a shipment skip from Pending directly to Delivered, bypassing In Transit? Spec says 'once in transit, it cannot be sent back to pending' but does not define the complete set of valid transitions.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Dispatchers","Drivers / Couriers"],"options":["A: Strict sequence only — Pending → In Transit → Delivered","B: Allow skip — Pending → Delivered also valid (e.g., for walk-in pickups)"],"recommendation":"Option A (strict sequence). In logistics, In Transit represents real physical movement and is operationally critical for tracking. Skipping it hides information."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No specification on whether shipment weight can be modified after creation, particularly once the shipment is In Transit or Delivered. Weight changes post-creation affect billing integrity.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Customers (shippers)"],"options":["A: Weight is immutable after creation","B: Weight is mutable only while Pending","C: Weight is always mutable (with re-billing)"],"recommendation":"Option B. Allow corrections while Pending (before physical handoff), lock after In Transit to preserve billing integrity."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
